musicalLanguage.description: Philip Glass's Opening (from Glassworks) appears here as a 22-measure excerpt at 122 BPM, and the harness flags a loud key mismatch: the stated A minor is poorly supported (statedKeyFit -0.341), while the content-based read, B major, clears a real margin (0.162). The 5 labeled chord windows (23% of windows considered) don't name B major directly, but they do support it diatonically — G# minor seventh (4 windows, the vi7 of B major) and C# minor seventh (1 window, the ii7 of B major) — though their confidence values are genuinely low (0.171 to 0.262), so this is honest, thin evidence rather than a confirmed progression. The piece's real strength is its ostinato construction: a 29-note left-hand figure recurs 4 times at a 3.515 compression ratio (measures 2-3, 4-5, 6-7, and 8-9), covering 41.3% of all left-hand events in the piece's first half, while several shorter right-hand cells layer on top through the second half. There is no written rest anywhere in these 22 measures, and five measures — 11, 13, 15, 20, and 22 — are exactly tied as the busiest, each carrying 26 onsets.
musicalLanguage.structure: The section lens finds only two internal boundaries across the excerpt (opening measures 6 and 16, the second carrying the piece's largest novelty at 3.462), dividing it into three brief regions. The first (measures 1-5) and second (6-15) regions are dominated by the 4-times-recurring 29-note left-hand ostinato, while a second, adjacent 53-note left-hand figure (measures 9-12 and 13-16) and its matching 29-note right-hand counterpart (measures 10-12 and 14-16) carry the piece into its third region (16-22), where the busiest, most tightly tied measures (20 and 22) sit.
keyMoments: The stated A minor is actively contradicted by the content (statedKeyFit -0.341), and while the content-based B major clears a real margin (0.162), the actual labeled windows support it only diatonically — G# minor seventh (the vi7 of B major, 4 windows) and C# minor seventh (the ii7, 1 window) — at genuinely low confidence (0.171 to 0.262), so treat B major as the best available reading, not a firmly confirmed one.

A 29-note left-hand ostinato recurs 4 times in immediate succession (measures 2-3, 4-5, 6-7, and 8-9) at a 3.515 compression ratio, covering 41.3% of all left-hand events — this single figure is the backbone of the excerpt's first half.
Five measures — 11, 13, 15, 20, and 22 — are exactly tied as the busiest in the piece, each carrying 26 onsets, spread across the second and third regions rather than concentrated in one spot.
There is no written rest anywhere in these 22 measures — every measure sounds in both hands, so this brief excerpt still demands sustained, continuous control from its very first bar.
teachingGoals: Master the 29-note left-hand ostinato (4 occurrences, 3.515 compression, 41.3% coverage) first — it is the single highest-value piece of material in this short excerpt.

Practice recognizing G# minor seventh and C# minor seventh as low-confidence but diatonically consistent support for the content-detected B major, rather than expecting a fully confirmed progression from such thin evidence.
Build even control across the tied-busiest measures (11, 13, 15, 20, and 22, all at 26 onsets), understanding this excerpt's intensity as spread rather than concentrated in one climax.

Learn the second-half material (the 53-note left-hand figure and its 29-note right-hand counterpart) as a related but distinct layer from the opening ostinato, since the pattern pass finds them in different, later measures.
styleTips: Pedal for resonance across the repeating left-hand ostinato — let the sustain accumulate a warm wash under the figure's four appearances rather than clearing it fully between repeats.
Play the tied-busiest measures with genuinely even weight — resist the instinct to build toward just one of them, since the data shows all five carry identical intensity.
Listen for the low-confidence G# minor seventh and C# minor seventh chords as color, not certainty — voice them lightly, matching the honestly thin evidence behind them.
Try building stamina for continuous playing from the very first measure, since there is no written rest anywhere in this short excerpt to rely on for recovery.
